records.title: The SpaceX IPO Could Trigger a Massive Rotation Across AI Stocks. Here's Why.
records.claim: SpaceX's record $1.77 trillion IPO on June 12 is drawing investor capital away from AI stocks. The article argues SpaceX's 139x revenue valuation is unsustainably high for an unprofitable company, and predicts its eventual decline could trigger a rotation back to AI leaders like Nvidia and Broadcom. Recent declines in major AI stocks suggest investors are taking profits to fund SpaceX purchases. The outcome will also influence upcoming IPOs from OpenAI and Anthropic.

records.title: AMD Is Up 149% Year to Date. Is the AI Chip Rally Getting Dangerously Crowded, or Is There Still Room to Run?
records.claim: AMD's stock has surged 149% year-to-date with Q1 2026 revenue reaching $10.3 billion (up 38% YoY) and data center revenue at $5.8 billion (up 57% YoY). The company guided for Q2 2026 revenue of $11.2 billion (46% YoY growth) and is preparing to launch MI450 AI chips with major customer commitments from OpenAI and Meta. Despite the stock's significant gains, the company's strong earnings growth and expanding AI market share suggest further upside potential for long-term investors.

records.title: This Shocking Company Just Passed Ford -- and Its Sales Could More Than Double
records.claim: BYD, a Chinese EV maker, has surpassed Ford in global automotive sales and become the world's top-selling EV maker. With 4.6 million vehicles sold and 7.7% growth, BYD's CEO claims the company can become the No. 1 global automaker within five years, driven by new Blade Battery 2.0 and Flash Charging technologies that have generated over 100,000 orders for some models.

records.title: SpaceX Stock Faces Post-IPO Valuation Risks as AST SpaceMobile, Rocket Lab Rebound
records.claim: SpaceX's IPO has driven its stock from $135 to over $225, creating a $2.5 trillion valuation driven by retail options trading and float scarcity rather than fundamentals. The company trades at 20x projected 2027 sales while posting $4.94 billion annual losses. A $60 billion all-stock acquisition of Cursor and Tesla merger rumors inflate valuations further. Meanwhile, AST SpaceMobile and Rocket Lab offer better risk-reward profiles after being temporarily overshadowed by the SpaceX IPO frenzy.

records.title: Is Intel the Comeback Stock of the Decade?
records.claim: Intel has experienced a remarkable sevenfold stock price increase over 14 months, rising from $18.84 to around $130 per share, driven by CEO Lip-Bu Tan's execution of the foundry strategy, the 18A process node success, CHIPS Act funding, and growing data center CPU demand. While other stocks like Carvana have achieved higher percentage returns, Intel's comeback is arguably more significant from a macro perspective as it strengthens U.S. semiconductor competitiveness and reduces dependence on Taiwan.

records.title: As Capital Rotates From Crypto to AI, Zoomex Traders Already Have Access to Both
records.claim: Zoomex, a crypto derivatives exchange, has launched Zoomex Stocks, offering tokenized versions of major U.S. equities and ETFs. The move reflects a significant market shift as institutional capital flows out of cryptocurrency into AI and semiconductor stocks, with Bitcoin ETFs experiencing $3.1 billion in year-to-date outflows while AI stocks surged 170%. The platform enables crypto traders to access equity exposure 24/7 using USDT with a flat 0.50% fee and near-instant settlement.

records.title: Prediction: This Could Be Nvidia's Stock Price By the End of 2027
records.claim: Nvidia could see its stock price reach $506 per share by end of 2027 based on CEO Jensen Huang's $1 trillion revenue visibility through 2027. The analysis projects 80% revenue growth in 2026 and 57% in 2027, driven by dominance in data center GPUs (85-92% market share) and rapid 12-18 month GPU generation cycles. At current valuations, Nvidia appears undervalued relative to its growth trajectory.

records.title: Custom AI Chips Are Coming for Nvidia's Crown. Here Are 2 Companies Quietly Cashing In.
records.claim: As major cloud companies design their own custom AI chips to reduce reliance on Nvidia, chip designers Broadcom and Marvell Technology are capturing significant revenue from this trend. Broadcom's AI revenue jumped 143% to $10.8 billion in its latest quarter with over $30 billion in booked AI orders, while Marvell's data center segment grew 27% with expectations for custom silicon to exceed $10 billion annually by fiscal 2029. Both companies trade at premium valuations but face customer concentration risks.

records.title: VoIP-Pal.com Inc. Engages Veteran Consumer Class Action and Complex Litigation Attorney C. Glen Ged as Strategic Legal Consultant
records.claim: VoIP-Pal.com Inc. has engaged C. Glen Ged, founder of Ged Lawyers LLP, as a strategic legal consultant to strengthen its litigation resources. The company is advancing four federal antitrust and consumer class action cases against major mobile carriers (AT&T, Verizon, T-Mobile) and platform providers (Apple, Google, Samsung) in the U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia. Ged will provide strategic advice and help identify qualified consumer class action firms to pursue the matters on a contingency-fee basis.

records.title: Elon Musk Just Delivered Fantastic News for ASML Stock Investors
records.claim: Elon Musk addressed ASML employees about the Terafab initiative, a joint venture between Tesla and SpaceX to build a massive semiconductor manufacturing complex with a proposed $55-119 billion investment. This signals strong future demand for ASML's essential EUV lithography machines used in AI chip production, validating the company's strategic importance to both companies' supply chains.

records.title: SpaceX Is Now Bigger Than Amazon and Closing in on Microsoft. Here's How to Think About the Valuation.
records.claim: SpaceX has become the fifth-largest company by market cap at $2.82 trillion following its IPO, surpassing Amazon and approaching Microsoft. The company's valuation is heavily driven by its AI division, which includes Grok, xAI, and planned orbital data centers. While Goldman Sachs projects 100-fold revenue growth by 2030, Morningstar assigns only a 7% probability to the company sustaining its current valuation, citing ambitious but unproven promises around orbital data centers and enterprise AI applications.

records.title: 3 Ways to Invest in Anthropic Before It Goes Public
records.claim: Anthropic, valued at $965 billion with $47 billion in annualized revenue, has confidentially filed for an IPO. Investors can gain indirect exposure through three public companies: Alphabet (committed to invest up to $40 billion and owns ~14%), Amazon (investing up to $25 billion total with planned $100 billion AWS spending), and Salesforce (owns ~$5 billion stake and integrates Claude into its AI platform).

records.title: Does the SpaceX IPO Mean You Should Ditch Rocket Lab?
records.claim: SpaceX's IPO has become the largest in history, causing investors to sell Rocket Lab shares, which dropped 32% from highs. While SpaceX has ambitious plans for AI data centers, Starlink, and Mars colonization, it trades at an extremely expensive 100+ times 2025 revenue. Rocket Lab, a smaller competitor focused on commercial rocket launches, is also expensive at 89x sales despite strong growth. The article recommends investors avoid both stocks due to their elevated valuations.

records.title: Is Microsoft a Deep Value Stock?
records.claim: Microsoft stock has declined nearly 20% year-to-date while tech peers rally, but the company's strong fundamentals remain intact. With AI business revenue at a $37 billion annual run rate (123% YoY growth) and a P/E ratio of 23.3—the second-lowest among Magnificent Seven stocks—Microsoft presents a potential deep value opportunity as cloud computing grows at a projected 16% CAGR through 2033.

records.title: 1 No-Brainer S&P 500 ETF to Buy Right Now for Less Than $500
records.claim: The Vanguard S&P 500 Growth ETF (VOOG) is recommended as an attractive investment option for new and small-stake investors following a 6-for-1 stock split in April that reduced its price to around $81. The fund tracks 146 S&P 500 growth stocks with a low expense ratio of 0.07% and offers significant exposure to technology and AI-related companies, though investors should be aware of concentration risks in the tech sector.

records.title: CoreWeave Stock Is Up Around 50% This Year, and Here's Why It Can Still Soar Higher
records.claim: CoreWeave (CRWV) has gained approximately 50% in 2026 and will be added to the Nasdaq-100 index on June 22, which is expected to drive further gains through increased fund exposure and investor interest. The company rents compute space and provides access to Nvidia's chips, benefiting from surging AI demand. However, despite revenue growth from $1.9B to $5.1B year-over-year, CoreWeave remains unprofitable with a $1.6B net loss in the trailing 12 months, making it a higher-risk investment.

records.title: Prediction: These 4 Words From Sam Altman Will Live on Infamy When It Comes to Artificial Intelligence
records.claim: The article warns investors about unrealistic expectations in AI stocks. OpenAI CEO Sam Altman cautioned that people are 'begging to be disappointed' by AI capabilities, suggesting current valuations of AI-exposed stocks may not be justified if growth expectations fall short. The author recommends considering more value-oriented stocks with predictable business models rather than overweighting AI stocks.

records.title: Nvidia's Jensen Huang Runs A $5 Trillion Giant Like It's 30 Days From Collapse. Perplexity's CEO Says That's The Secret— 'You Need To Work Forever…'
records.claim: Perplexity AI CEO Aravind Srinivas shared entrepreneurship lessons learned from Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang and Tesla CEO Elon Musk. Srinivas highlighted Huang's approach of operating with constant urgency despite Nvidia's $5 trillion valuation, and Musk's mission-driven compensation structure at SpaceX focused on Mars colonization rather than wealth accumulation. Srinivas rejected the FIRE philosophy, advocating for continuous work and purpose-driven entrepreneurship.

records.title: June 16 May be a Big Day for the Stock Market and Set the Stage for What Happens Next.
records.claim: New Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh will oversee his first FOMC meeting on June 16, 2026, amid rising inflation concerns. Consumer prices rose 4.2% in May, the highest in three years, potentially complicating rate-cut expectations. While the Fed is expected to leave rates unchanged at this meeting, Warsh's policy direction—including potential balance sheet reduction and less forward guidance—could significantly impact the stock market, particularly given historically expensive valuations.

records.title: Meta to Follow Alphabet’s Footsteps? What an Equity Raise Could Mean
records.claim: Meta is reportedly considering an equity raise of tens of billions of dollars to fund AI infrastructure, potentially following Alphabet's $84.75 billion offering. However, Meta would face significantly higher shareholder dilution (~4.3%) compared to Alphabet (~1.9%) to raise a similar amount, which could negatively impact existing shareholders. While the capital could accelerate Meta's AI product development, the dilution effect and market reaction remain concerns.

records.title: Meta Platforms Is Quietly Becoming One of the Market's Best AI Stocks to Buy. Here Are 3 Reasons Why.
records.claim: Meta's AI capabilities are significantly boosting its core advertising business, with Q1 revenue rising 33% year-over-year driven by AI-improved content ranking and ad optimization. The company can rapidly deploy AI features to 3.5 billion daily users, and its massive $125-145 billion capital expenditure for AI infrastructure is funded by strong profitability. Trading at 22x earnings, Meta appears attractively valued despite risks from delayed developer APIs and nascent monetization of AI agents.

records.title: Over 200 Crypto Firms Now Back the Clarity Act. If It Passes, This 1 Cryptocurrency Could Soar in Price
records.claim: A coalition of over 200 crypto firms is urging the Senate to pass the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act, which would establish a federal regulatory framework for digital assets and clarify SEC/CFTC roles. If passed, Solana could benefit significantly as it would likely be reclassified as a digital commodity regulated by the CFTC rather than the SEC, potentially attracting more investors and yield-seeking participants.

records.title: Roku Is Being Acquired. Here's What Investors Need to Know.
records.claim: Fox Corporation has agreed to acquire Roku in a $22 billion cash-and-stock deal valued at $160 per share. The acquisition combines Fox's live news and sports portfolio with Roku's streaming platform, positioning the combined entity as a major player in connected TV. However, both stocks declined on the announcement, with Fox down 16% and Roku down 1%, as investors expressed concerns about Fox taking on $12 billion in new debt and the 34% premium paid for Roku.

records.title: Warren Buffett Successor Greg Abel Pours $10 Billion More Into His Largest Bet Yet
records.claim: Berkshire Hathaway CEO Greg Abel has invested $21 billion total in Alphabet shares, with $11 billion in Q1 and an additional $10 billion recently negotiated at a discount. This investment marks a significant shift from Warren Buffett's tech-averse strategy, positioning Alphabet as Berkshire's fourth-largest holding. The capital will fund AI infrastructure and employee compensation, supporting Google Cloud's accelerating growth and AI-driven improvements in the core advertising business.

records.title: Bull Market Pullback: Why the 4.5% Dip Held the 50-DMA
records.claim: The S&P 500 experienced a 4.5% pullback from its May 27 record high of 7,621 to 7,248 (the 50-day moving average), which the author characterizes as a healthy bull market correction rather than the start of a bear market. The bounce back to 7,431.46 on broad participation and positive money flows suggests the uptrend remains intact. However, the author warns of underlying risks including record margin debt ($1.30 trillion), declining retail demand, and inverted equity risk premium as 10-year Treasuries now yield more than S&P 500 earnings. The portfolio remains at 100% equity exposure based on positive money flow signals, but disciplined risk management is maintained ahead of historically weak summer months and midterm election volatility.

records.title: Box CEO Aaron Levie Says AI Restrictions Could Accelerate Global Shift To Open-Weight Models
records.claim: Box CEO Aaron Levie warns that AI access restrictions and regulatory uncertainty are pushing governments and businesses toward open-weight AI models and sovereign AI development. He argues that regulatory focus on the model layer rather than applied layer creates geopolitical risks and incentivizes countries to develop independent AI systems, with open-weight models likely to benefit most as they are generally not U.S.-controlled.

records.title: Warren Buffett's Successor, Greg Abel, Is Betting Big on a Virtual Monopoly That's About to Become Berkshire Hathaway's 4th-Largest Holding
records.claim: Greg Abel, Warren Buffett's successor as Berkshire Hathaway CEO, is significantly increasing the company's investment in Alphabet, making it Berkshire's 4th-largest holding. Berkshire committed $10 billion to Alphabet's $84.75 billion stock offering to fund AI infrastructure expansion. Abel is shifting Berkshire's strategy toward tech stocks, recognizing Alphabet's dominant position in search advertising and its emerging role as a leading AI applications deployer.

records.title: Nvidia's China Woes Deepen As TikTok Parent ByteDance Eyes AI Chips From Iluvatar CoreX, Baidu: Report
records.claim: ByteDance is in talks with Chinese chipmakers Iluvatar CoreX and Baidu to acquire AI chips, with Iluvatar CoreX projected to deliver 50,000 chips this year. This development reflects how Chinese tech firms are building domestic AI chip alternatives amid U.S. export restrictions on Nvidia, with Chinese GPU manufacturers now claiming nearly 41% of the domestic AI accelerator server market.

records.title: The SpaceX IPO Has Wall Street Debating Whether the AI Boom Is a Bubble. Both Sides Have a Point.
records.claim: SpaceX's record $75 billion IPO has sparked debate about whether the AI boom is a bubble. Bears point to stretched valuations (S&P 500 cyclically adjusted P/E near 40, SpaceX trading at 90x revenue) and shrinking free cash flow at major tech companies despite massive AI spending. Bulls counter that massive cloud backlogs and unmet demand justify the investment, citing successful past technology cycles.
